_comment: Per-department question banks for the Brainstorming Buddy role (v12.1.0). Each entry: dept_deliverable (noun), build_specialists_str (SOP 9.4 / Section 11 handoff list), rendered_question_bank (markdown injected into {{DEPT_QUESTION_BANK}} in the template).

presentations.build_specialists_str: Hook Strategist, Slide Copywriter, Offer/Price Strategist, Brand Steward, Slide Image Creator, QC Specialist, Slide Submitter, Media Librarian/GHL Updater, PPTX Assembly Specialist, Presenter Coach, Delivery Concierge
presentations.rendered_question_bank:
### OPENING (always, before mode offer)

- O1. "In one line, what is this presentation FOR -- what do you want people to do at the end?" -> `GOAL`, `CTA_ACTION`
- O2. "Is this a live webinar pitch, a teaching deck, a sales deck, or something else?" -> `DECK_TYPE`

### SIMPLE (7 or fewer)

1. THE GOAL -- what action at the end (buy / book / join / enroll). `GOAL`, `CTA_ACTION`
2. THE FEELING -- how should they feel walking away. `TARGET_FEELING`
3. THE TONE -- pick one of the seven named styles (Inspirational / Tough Love / Challenger / Teacher / Storyteller / High-Energy Hype / Calm Premium) or blend two. `TONE`
4. THE OFFER + PRICE -- what are you selling and at what final price; gradual price drop or straight price. `OFFER_STACK`, `FINAL_PRICE`, `PRICE_MODE`
5. DURATION -- how many minutes (10/15/30/45/60/90). `DURATION_MIN`
6. AUDIENCE -- who is it for, and how should the people in the images break down (with percentages). `AUDIENCE`, `REPRESENTATION_MIX`
7. BRAND LOOK -- brand colors / logo on slides yes-no (skip if on file). `BRAND_PRIMARY`, `LOGO_ON_SLIDES`

### EXTENSIVE (10 to 20) -- simple set PLUS

8. THE HOOK SEED -- one line you already say all the time you want them humming. `HOOK`
9. PRICE STRUCTURE DETAIL -- full offer stack, each component's standalone value, the anchor (>= 3x final). `PRICE_ANCHOR`, `PAYMENT_PLAN`
10. VIP / PREMIUM TIER -- want one; what it includes; real spot count. `VIP_TIER`, `VIP_PRICE`, `VIP_SPOTS`
11. PRIMARY OBJECTION -- the #1 reason people say no. `OBJECTION`
12. PROOF ASSETS -- testimonials, screenshots, press logos, before/after numbers (collect now). `PROOF_ASSETS`
13. VISUAL MIX -- people-heavy / some-people / typography-led / mix. `VISUAL_MIX`
14. STYLE REFERENCES -- decks or brands they admire. `STYLE_PREFS`
15. TRANSFORMATION PROMISE -- the before/after the offer delivers. `TRANSFORMATION_PROMISE`
16. SLIDE COUNT PREFERENCE -- a target, or let the duration math decide. `SLIDE_COUNT`
17. DARK STYLING OK -- default false. `DARK_OK`
18. DELIVERY DESTINATIONS -- where the final deck goes (GHL / Drive / local). `DELIVERY_DESTINATIONS`
19. DEADLINE -- when they need it live. `DEADLINE`
20. ANYTHING ELSE important, captured verbatim. `CLIENT_NOTES`

video.build_specialists_str: Storyboard/Pre-Production Specialist, Video Editor, Long-Form Specialist, Short-Form Specialist, Motion Graphics Specialist, VSL Specialist, AI Video Generator Specialist, Color Grading Specialist, Captioning/Subtitling Specialist, Video SEO Specialist
video.rendered_question_bank:
### OPENING (always, before mode offer)

- O1. "In one line, what is this video and where will it live (YouTube / Reel-Short-TikTok / VSL / ad / course module)?" -> `VIDEO_TYPE`, `PLATFORM`
- O2. "What do you want a viewer to do after watching?" -> `GOAL`, `CTA`

### SIMPLE (7 or fewer)

1. VIDEO TYPE + PLATFORM -- long-form / short-form / VSL / explainer / testimonial; where it posts. `VIDEO_TYPE`, `PLATFORM`
2. GOAL -- the action / outcome. `GOAL`, `CTA`
3. LENGTH -- target runtime (seconds for short-form, minutes for long-form). `RUNTIME`
4. CORE MESSAGE -- the one thing the viewer must remember. `KEY_MESSAGE`
5. TONE / STYLE -- talking-head / screen-recording / cinematic / animated / mixed; energy level. `STYLE`, `ENERGY`
6. SOURCE MATERIAL -- do you have footage/script, or are we creating from scratch (AI video). `HAS_FOOTAGE`, `MODE`
7. AUDIENCE -- who is watching. `AUDIENCE`

### EXTENSIVE (10 to 20) -- simple set PLUS

8. HOOK -- the first 3 seconds; what stops the scroll. `HOOK`
9. STORYBOARD ARC -- problem -> solution -> proof -> CTA, or a specific narrative they want. `ARC`
10. ASPECT RATIO -- 16:9 / 9:16 / 1:1. `ASPECT_RATIO`
11. CAPTIONS / SUBTITLES -- needed; burned-in style preference. `CAPTIONS`, `CAPTION_STYLE`
12. MUSIC / VOICEOVER -- AI voice or owner VO; music vibe. `VOICE`, `MUSIC_VIBE`
13. B-ROLL / VISUALS -- footage sources, brand assets, on-screen text moments. `BROLL`, `ONSCREEN_TEXT`
14. BRAND ELEMENTS -- logo, lower-thirds, color, intro/outro. `BRAND_ELEMENTS`
15. PROOF / METRICS to show on screen. `PROOF_POINTS`
16. SEO (long-form) -- title, target keyword, thumbnail concept. `VIDEO_SEO`, `THUMBNAIL_CONCEPT`
17. SERIES OR ONE-OFF -- is this part of a recurring format. `SERIES`
18. DELIVERY DESTINATIONS + format. `DELIVERY_DESTINATIONS`
19. DEADLINE. `DEADLINE`
20. ANYTHING ELSE verbatim. `NOTES`

graphics.build_specialists_str: AI Image Generator Specialist, Ad Creative Specialist, Brand Identity Specialist, Social Media Graphics Specialist, Infographic Specialist, Thumbnail/Cover Designer, Presentation Designer, Email Designer, Print/Asset Design Specialist, Book Cover Designer, Course Slide Designer, Style Analyst, Deck Systems Specialist, Generation Operator, Photo Shoot Director, Fidelity Tester
graphics.rendered_question_bank:
### OPENING (always, before mode offer)

- O1. "In one line, what graphic do you need (logo / ad creative / social post / thumbnail / infographic / book cover / print piece / slide design)?" -> `GRAPHIC_TYPE`
- O2. "Where will it be used?" -> `PLACEMENT`

### SIMPLE (7 or fewer)

1. GRAPHIC TYPE + PLACEMENT -- what and where. `GRAPHIC_TYPE`, `PLACEMENT`
2. PURPOSE -- what it needs to accomplish (stop the scroll / explain / brand / sell). `PURPOSE`
3. KEY MESSAGE / TEXT -- the words on it, if any. `HEADLINE`, `BODY_TEXT`
4. BRAND -- colors, fonts, logo (skip if on file). `BRAND_COLORS`, `BRAND_FONTS`, `LOGO`
5. SIZE / DIMENSIONS -- platform spec or print size. `DIMENSIONS`
6. STYLE / MOOD -- clean / bold / luxury / playful / editorial; references. `STYLE`, `REFERENCES`
7. AUDIENCE -- who sees it. `AUDIENCE`

### EXTENSIVE (10 to 20) -- simple set PLUS

8. QUANTITY / SET -- one graphic or a set/variants (e.g. 5 ad sizes). `QUANTITY`, `SIZE_SET`
9. COPY HIERARCHY -- what must be largest / most prominent. `HIERARCHY`
10. IMAGERY -- photo / illustration / AI-generated / icon-based; people in it. `IMAGERY_TYPE`, `PEOPLE_IN_IMAGE`
11. COLOR CONSTRAINTS -- must-use / must-avoid colors. `COLOR_RULES`
12. CALL TO ACTION -- the action prompt on the graphic. `CTA`
13. INSPIRATION -- competitor or admired examples. `INSPO`
14. ACCESSIBILITY -- contrast / legibility requirements. `A11Y`
15. FILE FORMATS needed -- PNG / SVG / PDF / source. `FORMATS`
16. PART OF A CAMPAIGN -- ties to a larger marketing push. `CAMPAIGN_LINK`
17. REVISIONS expected / variants to test. `VARIANTS`
18. DELIVERY DESTINATIONS. `DELIVERY_DESTINATIONS`
19. DEADLINE. `DEADLINE`
20. ANYTHING ELSE verbatim. `NOTES`

### DIU-SPECIFIC (extensive set, items 21-27 -- ask when applicable)

21. STYLE REFERENCES -- "Do you have reference images, an existing deck, or past designs whose LOOK you want reused or analyzed?" -> `STYLE_REFERENCES`, `ANALYZE_REQUEST`
22. STYLE LIBRARY ID -- "Should this reuse a saved style from your style library (give me the ID if you know it), or is this a brand-new look?" -> `STYLE_ID`, `NEW_STYLE`
23. PEOPLE / LIKENESS -- "Will a real person appear in the image (you, your team, a client)? If yes, whose likeness, and do we have their reference photos on file?" -> `PEOPLE_LIKENESS`, `IDENTITY_REFS`
24. DECK SPECS -- "For decks: how many slides, and what resolution does the final need (screen-share default, or print/projection quality)?" -> `SLIDE_COUNT`, `RESOLUTION_CHOICE`
25. FIDELITY PRIORITY -- "How important is exact style match? Is this a critical brand deliverable (we need a tight fidelity score) or an exploratory first pass (directionally right is enough)?" -> `FIDELITY_PRIORITY` (values: critical / standard / exploratory)
26. STYLE AVOID -- "Are there any looks, colors, or visual elements you want to absolutely avoid in this image -- things that are off-brand or have tested badly before?" -> `STYLE_AVOID`, `NEGATIVE_NOTES`
27. SHOOT MODE -- "If this is a personal photo shoot or branded lifestyle image: what is the scene context -- location, wardrobe, action, editorial portrait, or a retouching pass on an existing photo?" -> `SHOOT_MODE` (values: location / wardrobe / action / editorial / retouch)

marketing.build_specialists_str: Funnel Strategist, Content Strategist / Content Marketing Strategist, Brand Positioning Specialist, Email Campaign Strategist, Lead Magnet Specialist, Customer Journey Architect, Webinar/Event Marketing Specialist, Affiliate/Referral Specialist, Influencer Marketing Specialist, Marketing Analytics Specialist
marketing.rendered_question_bank:
### OPENING (always, before mode offer)

- O1. "In one line, what are you trying to achieve with this campaign (launch / lead-gen / nurture / promo / awareness)?" -> `CAMPAIGN_GOAL`
- O2. "What are you promoting?" -> `OFFER`

### SIMPLE (7 or fewer)

1. CAMPAIGN GOAL -- the single measurable outcome. `CAMPAIGN_GOAL`, `SUCCESS_METRIC`
2. OFFER / PRODUCT -- what is being marketed and its price. `OFFER`, `PRICE`
3. TARGET AUDIENCE -- who, and their #1 pain. `AUDIENCE`, `PAIN`
4. CHANNELS -- email / social / paid / content / webinar (pick the main ones). `CHANNELS`
5. CORE MESSAGE -- the hook / positioning in one line. `CORE_MESSAGE`
6. BUDGET -- spend ceiling, if any (drives paid vs organic). `BUDGET`
7. TIMELINE -- launch date / campaign window. `TIMELINE`

### EXTENSIVE (10 to 20) -- simple set PLUS

8. FUNNEL STAGE -- top (awareness) / middle (nurture) / bottom (convert). `FUNNEL_STAGE`
9. OFFER STACK + value justification. `OFFER_STACK`, `VALUE_PROPS`
10. PRIMARY OBJECTION to overcome. `OBJECTION`
11. DESIRED CUSTOMER JOURNEY -- entry to conversion steps. `JOURNEY`
12. LEAD MAGNET -- is there a freebie/entry offer. `LEAD_MAGNET`
13. EMAIL SEQUENCE needed -- how many touches. `EMAIL_TOUCHES`
14. PROOF / SOCIAL PROOF available. `PROOF`
15. COMPETITORS / positioning -- who else and how we differ. `COMPETITORS`, `DIFFERENTIATOR`
16. CTA + landing destination (ties to Web dept). `CTA`, `LANDING_PAGE`
17. KPIs to track -- CPL / CAC / conversion / ROAS. `KPIS`
18. ASSETS needed from sibling depts (graphics, video). `ASSET_NEEDS`
19. BRAND VOICE constraints. `BRAND_VOICE`
20. ANYTHING ELSE verbatim. `NOTES`

web-development.build_specialists_str: Web Designer, Frontend/JavaScript/React Specialist, Landing Page Specialist, Funnel Builder Specialist, WordPress Specialist, Member Area Specialist, CRO Specialist, SEO Specialist, Technical SEO Specialist, Web Accessibility Specialist, Web Security Specialist
web-development.rendered_question_bank:
### OPENING (always, before mode offer)

- O1. "In one line, what are you building (a website / landing page / funnel / membership site / mobile app / web app / desktop app)?" -> `BUILD_TYPE`
- O2. "What is the single most important thing a user should be able to do?" -> `PRIMARY_ACTION`

### SIMPLE (7 or fewer)

1. BUILD TYPE -- site / funnel / membership / mobile app / web app. `BUILD_TYPE`
2. PRIMARY GOAL -- the one action / outcome (book / buy / sign up / use feature). `PRIMARY_ACTION`, `GOAL`
3. AUDIENCE / USERS -- who uses it. `USERS`
4. KEY PAGES OR SCREENS -- the must-have pages/screens. `PAGES_OR_SCREENS`
5. BRAND / DESIGN -- existing brand, colors, references. `BRAND`, `DESIGN_REFS`
6. PLATFORM / TECH PREFERENCE -- WordPress / GHL funnel / custom / iOS / Android / PWA (or "you choose"). `PLATFORM`
7. TIMELINE. `TIMELINE`

### EXTENSIVE (10 to 20) -- simple set PLUS

8. CORE FEATURES / FUNCTIONALITY -- the feature list. `FEATURES`
9. USER FLOWS -- the main journeys (signup -> onboarding -> core action). `USER_FLOWS`
10. CONTENT -- who provides copy/images; is it ready. `CONTENT_SOURCE`
11. INTEGRATIONS -- payments (Stripe), CRM (GHL), email, analytics, auth. `INTEGRATIONS`
12. DATA -- what data is stored; accounts/login needed. `DATA_MODEL`, `AUTH_NEEDED`
13. CONVERSION GOALS / CRO -- what conversion to optimize (web). `CONVERSION_GOAL`
14. SEO -- target keywords, organic priority (web). `SEO_TARGETS`
15. RESPONSIVE / DEVICES -- mobile-first, desktop, native app stores. `DEVICES`
16. ACCESSIBILITY + SECURITY requirements. `A11Y`, `SECURITY`
17. EXISTING ASSETS -- current site/app to migrate or start fresh. `EXISTING_ASSET`, `MIGRATION`
18. SUCCESS METRICS -- what "working" looks like. `SUCCESS_METRICS`
19. BUDGET / SCOPE constraints. `SCOPE`
20. ANYTHING ELSE verbatim. `NOTES`

app-development.build_specialists_str: UX/UI Specialist, API/Backend Specialist, iOS Specialist, Android Specialist, PWA Specialist, Desktop App Specialist, Cloud Infrastructure Specialist, App Analytics Specialist, ASO Specialist, QA Tester
